title: NBA Daily Rundown: Playoff Thrillers and Star Power!
subtitle: May 03, 2026 Edition

items.headline: 76ers Stun Celtics in Game 7 Thriller, Advance to Second Round!
items.summary: The Philadelphia 76ers, a No. 7 seed, completed a historic 3-1 comeback against the No. 2 seed Boston Celtics, securing a 109-100 victory in Game 7 of their Eastern Conference first-round playoff series. Joel Embiid dominated with 34 points, 12 rebounds, and 6 assists, while Tyrese Maxey added a stellar 30 points, 11 rebounds, and 7 assists in a historic performance. Boston played without star Jayson Tatum, sidelined due to left knee stiffness. Philadelphia will now face the New York Knicks in the Eastern Conference Semifinals.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Superstar Leap Defines Blazers' Playoff Breakthrough
items.summary: Portland Trail Blazers' Israeli forward Deni Avdija has made a significant 'superstar leap' this season, earning an All-Star selection and emerging as a top contender for the 2026 Most Improved Player award. Avdija concluded his playoff run averaging 22.2 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 4.6 assists in his first playoff appearance. He previously delivered a historic 41-point, 12-assist performance in a Play-In win against the Suns. Avdija also had a noteworthy on-court scuffle with Spurs rookie Stephon Castle during their series.

items.headline: 76ers @ Celtics - Game 7
items.summary: The 76ers secured a monumental Game 7 victory over the Celtics to advance.

items.headline: Lakers @ Rockets
items.summary: The Los Angeles Lakers defeated the Houston Rockets to wrap up their Western Conference first-round series, advancing to the next round.

items.headline: Magic @ Pistons - Game 7
items.summary: The Magic and Pistons face off in a decisive Game 7. Orlando's Franz Wagner is out, and Jonathan Isaac is doubtful, while Detroit's Kevin Huerter is questionable.

items.headline: Raptors @ Cavaliers - Game 7
items.summary: The Raptors and Cavaliers battle in a winner-take-all Game 7.

items.headline: Joel Embiid Dominates Game 7
items.statContext: Led 76ers to series victory over Celtics.

items.headline: Tyrese Maxey's Game 7 Heroics
items.statContext: Joined Embiid with an incredible Game 7 stat line.

items.headline: Rookie VJ Edgecombe Steps Up Big
items.statContext: Set a 76ers rookie record for points in a Game 7.

items.headline: Jaylen Brown's Valiant Effort
items.statContext: Led Boston in scoring in their Game 7 loss.

items.headline: Deni Avdija's Breakout Season
items.statContext: Averaged these numbers in his first playoff appearance.

items.headline: 76ers Set to Face Knicks in Eastern Conference Semifinals
items.summary: After their Game 7 triumph, the Philadelphia 76ers will now travel to New York to face the third-seeded Knicks in Game 1 of their second-round series on Monday.

items.headline: Lakers Advance to Face Thunder in Western Semifinals
items.summary: With their first-round series concluded, the Los Angeles Lakers will advance to the Western Conference Semifinals, where they will take on the top-seeded Oklahoma City Thunder. Game 1 is scheduled for Tuesday.

items.headline: Durant to Nuggets? Davis on the Move? Trade Rumors Swirl
items.summary: Speculation is heating up around a potential blockbuster trade that could see Kevin Durant teaming up with Nikola Jokic in Denver, while Jamal Murray might be headed to the Houston Rockets. Additionally, Anthony Davis's future with the Washington Wizards is uncertain, with reports suggesting he could be traded.

items.headline: Key Injuries Impacting Playoff Matchups
items.summary: Beyond Jayson Tatum's absence in Game 7, other star players are dealing with injuries. Orlando Magic's Franz Wagner is out and Jonathan Isaac is doubtful for today's Game 7 against the Pistons, who list Kevin Huerter as questionable.

items.headline: Embiid Pledges to Support 76ers Home Crowd for Knicks Series
items.summary: Philadelphia's Joel Embiid has offered to pay for 76ers fans' playoff tickets to ensure a strong home-court advantage against the New York Knicks in their upcoming series, aiming to prevent visiting fans from dominating the arena.
